threat_model.name: SecureTrade Financial Services Multi-Cloud STRIDE + MITRE ATT&CK Threat Model
threat_model.methodology: STRIDE + MITRE ATT&CK for Enterprise v14.1 + Financial Sector Threat Taxonomy

trust_boundaries.name: Public Internet
trust_boundaries.description: Trader browsers, mobile apps, algorithmic trading clients, external market data consumers

trust_boundaries.name: Edge Layer (Cloudflare)
trust_boundaries.description: Cloudflare Workers edge compute, WAF, DNS, load balancing. JWT validation and geo-routing at edge

trust_boundaries.name: Application Tier (GKE/EKS)
trust_boundaries.description: GKE Autopilot (primary) + EKS (DR) running Spring Boot, Go, Python microservices with Istio mTLS mesh

trust_boundaries.name: Data Tier (Spanner/AlloyDB/BigQuery)
trust_boundaries.description: Cloud Spanner global transactions, AlloyDB analytics, BigQuery warehouse, Memorystore Redis cache

trust_boundaries.name: External Payment and Market Services
trust_boundaries.description: Bloomberg B-PIPE, SWIFT Alliance, DTCC CTM, FINRA TRACE, Stripe, Plaid connections

trust_boundaries.name: DR Region (AWS us-east-1)
trust_boundaries.description: EKS failover cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL replica, AWS Secrets Manager, KMS CMK

trust_boundaries.name: Monitoring and Audit Plane
trust_boundaries.description: Chronicle SIEM, Security Command Center, Cloud Monitoring, Grafana, audit trail WORM storage

threats.description: Server-Side Request Forgery via Next.js Server Actions (CVE-2024-34351). An attacker crafts a malicious Host header in a POST request to a Next.js Server Action. The framework follows the attacker-controlled Host in a server-side redirect, allowing the attacker to reach the GCP metadata endpoint (metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token). The stolen Compute Engine service account token provides Spanner Admin, KMS Decrypt, and BigQuery Data Viewer roles. Attacker can read the entire trade ledger and decrypt PAN tokens.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $125M+ -- full GCP project compromise, PAN exposure triggers PCI Level 1 breach notification, SOX material weakness
mitigations: Upgrade Next.js to >=14.1.1 (patches CVE-2024-34351 Host header validation)
Deploy GCP metadata concealment on all GKE nodes (--metadata=disable-legacy-endpoints=true)
Restrict Workload Identity: Next.js pods get read-only roles, not Spanner Admin
Cloudflare Workers: strip and replace Host header before forwarding to GKE
Network egress policy: deny egress to 169.254.169.254 from application pods

threats.description: Insider threat (rogue developer with production Spanner Writer IAM role) directly modifies historical trade records via gcloud spanner databases execute-sql. Tampered records alter P&L attribution, falsifying SOX financial statements. Spanner's strong consistency guarantees mean the tampered data propagates globally within 10ms, including to the DR Aurora replica via the sync pipeline. Audit Trail Service logs are also overwritten if the insider has write access to the WORM bucket configuration.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $250M+ -- SOX material misstatement, SEC enforcement action, potential criminal wire fraud charges
mitigations: Enforce Spanner Fine-Grained Access Control (FGAC) with column-level restrictions on trade_price, quantity, counterparty
Enable Spanner Data Boost for read-only analytics queries, blocking direct SQL mutations from analytics roles
SOX ITGC: two-person approval for any Spanner IAM binding changes (Terraform plan requires dual sign-off)
Cryptographic hash chain on trade records (SHA-256 Merkle tree stored in separate immutable ledger)
Cloud Audit Logs DATA_WRITE export to WORM-configured GCS bucket in separate GCP project

threats.description: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints exposed without authentication on the trading engine (CVE-2023-20873, Spring Boot 3.0.x-3.0.6 Actuator bypass). An attacker inside the Istio mesh exploits a compromised low-privilege pod to reach /actuator/env, extracting database connection strings and Cloud KMS key references. Then uses /actuator/shutdown to halt the trading engine during market hours, or /actuator/configprops to leak all @Value-injected secrets including Bloomberg API credentials.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $75M -- trading halt during market hours (lost revenue + regulatory penalty), credential exposure
mitigations: Disable all Spring Boot Actuator endpoints except /health and /info in production profiles
Apply Spring Security to Actuator endpoints: management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info
Upgrade Spring Boot to >=3.2.5 if running vulnerable Actuator bypass versions
Istio AuthorizationPolicy: deny access to /actuator/** from all pods except monitoring namespace
Strip Actuator JAR from production container image via Maven shade plugin exclusion

threats.description: Risk approval decisions are logged to local filesystem (/var/log/risk-approvals/) inside the Go risk engine container. When the pod is evicted or restarted by GKE Autopilot autoscaling, all approval logs are lost. A risk officer can then claim they never approved a trade that exceeded VaR limits, and the firm cannot produce the approval record for FINRA Rule 4370 (Business Continuity) or SEC Rule 17a-4 (Records Retention). Non-repudiation failure for regulatory audit.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $15M -- FINRA fine for records retention failure + potential license restrictions
mitigations: Emit risk approval events to Cloud Pub/Sub synchronously before returning HTTP 200 (at-least-once delivery)
Audit Trail Service persists approvals to Spanner with cryptographic signatures (RSA-SHA256)
Mount GCS FUSE volume for risk approval logs with WORM retention policy (7 years)
FIDO2/WebAuthn step-up authentication for high-value risk approvals (cryptographic proof of identity)
OpenTelemetry trace correlation: bind risk approval span_id to trade execution span_id

threats.description: EKS cluster in AWS us-east-1 has the Kubernetes API server public endpoint enabled (default EKS configuration). An attacker who obtains a valid kubeconfig (leaked in CI/CD logs, developer laptop compromise, or GitHub Actions secret exposure) can enumerate all Kubernetes Secrets via kubectl get secrets -A. Secrets contain AlloyDB passwords, Plaid client_secret, Stripe API keys, Bloomberg BLPAPI credentials, and SWIFT HSM PINs. PCI CDE credentials are directly exposed.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $50M -- PCI Level 1 breach (cardholder data exposure), SWIFT CSP compliance failure
mitigations: Disable EKS public API endpoint: set endpointPublicAccess=false, use VPN for cluster access
Enable EKS Secrets encryption with AWS KMS CMK (EncryptionConfig on api-server)
Migrate all secrets to AWS Secrets Manager with CSI Secret Store Driver (no etcd storage)
IRSA (IAM Roles for Service Accounts) for all pods -- eliminate long-lived AWS credentials
Kubernetes RBAC: deny secrets/list and secrets/get for all roles except sealed-secrets-controller

threats.mitre_technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Web Session Cookie
threats.description: Redis 7.2 cluster configured without ACL authentication (AUTH disabled by default in Memorystore basic tier). Any pod in the GKE VPC can connect to Redis port 6379 and execute KEYS *, dumping all trader session tokens. Attacker injects a forged session for a senior trader account with role=admin, then uses that session to execute unauthorized block trades through the Trading Engine. The forged session bypasses the Auth/Identity Service because session validation trusts Redis as the source of truth.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $85M -- unauthorized trading losses, FINRA Rule 3110 supervisory failure, GLBA privacy breach
mitigations: Enable Memorystore AUTH with Redis ACL (requirepass + per-user ACL rules)
Enable in-transit TLS encryption on Memorystore (transit_encryption_mode=SERVER_AUTHENTICATION)
Restrict Redis access via VPC firewall rules: only trading-engine and auth-service pod CIDR ranges
Implement session token HMAC validation in the Auth Service (detect tampered session payloads)
Disable Redis DEBUG, MONITOR, CONFIG, KEYS commands via ACL (prevent enumeration)

threats.description: Cloudflare Worker JWT validation uses a cached JWKS endpoint. If the JWKS cache TTL expires (default 60s) and the origin JWKS endpoint is temporarily unreachable (GKE downscale, network partition), the Worker falls back to accepting tokens without signature verification for 30 seconds (fail-open design). An attacker monitoring the edge for this window can present a self-signed JWT with arbitrary claims (role=admin, trader_id=CEO), bypassing all authentication. The 30-second window is sufficient to submit high-value trade orders.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $40M -- unauthorized trades during auth bypass window, regulatory investigation
mitigations: Change JWT validation to fail-closed: reject all requests if JWKS is unreachable (return 503)
Cache JWKS with 24-hour TTL and background refresh (never let cache expire during request)
Pin JWKS public key in Worker KV store as fallback (eliminate network dependency)
Implement JWT audience (aud) and issuer (iss) validation in addition to signature
Alert on JWKS fetch failures via Cloudflare Workers Analytics (detect the vulnerability window)

threats.description: Pub/Sub topic 'trade-events' does not enforce message schema validation. A compromised service (or attacker with pubsub.topics.publish IAM role) publishes malformed trade event messages with negative quantities or zero prices. The Settlement Service consumes these events and initiates settlement instructions to DTCC with falsified quantities. The T+1 settlement window means errors propagate to clearing houses before detection, requiring costly trade breaks and FINRA regulatory reporting.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $30M -- trade break costs, DTCC penalties, FINRA Rule 11890 clearly erroneous trade claims
mitigations: Enable Pub/Sub schema validation with Protocol Buffers schema (reject malformed messages at publish time)
HMAC signing of trade events at the Trading Engine (verify signature at Settlement Service)
IAM: restrict pubsub.topics.publish to only the Trading Engine service account
Schema evolution policy: only backward-compatible changes, reviewed via Terraform PR
Dead-letter topic with alerting for any rejected messages (detect injection attempts)

threats.mitre_technique_name: Endpoint Denial of Service: Service Exhaustion Flood
threats.description: Algorithmic trading API abuse: a malicious or misconfigured algo trading client sends 50,000 order requests per second to the Apigee X gateway. The API Gateway's default quota policy (10,000 req/min per developer app) is insufficient for a distributed attack using multiple API keys. Apigee's connection pool to the GKE backend exhausts, causing 502 Bad Gateway for all legitimate traders during peak market hours. Best execution obligation violated (FINRA Rule 5310).
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $20M -- missed trading opportunities, best execution violations, regulatory fine
mitigations: Implement per-IP and per-user rate limiting at Cloudflare Workers (before traffic reaches Apigee)
Apigee SpikeArrest policy: max 100 orders/second per trader account with exponential backoff response
FINRA 15c3-5 kill switch: auto-halt all order processing if aggregate rate exceeds 10x baseline
Apigee target server connection pooling: max 500 connections with circuit breaker (fail fast to client)
GKE HPA with aggressive scale-up (50% CPU threshold) for trading engine pods during market hours

threats.description: Model poisoning via adversarial training data injection. An attacker with write access to the Vertex AI Feature Store injects carefully crafted training samples that cause the fraud detection model to learn a blind spot for a specific transaction pattern (e.g., round-lot trades from a specific IP range always classified as 'legitimate'). The model is retrained on the poisoned data during the weekly training pipeline, and the poisoned model is deployed via A/B test. Fraudulent transactions matching the blind spot pattern are no longer flagged, enabling sustained card-not-present fraud.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $60M -- undetected fraud losses over model lifetime, PCI DSS 6.2.4 integrity failure
mitigations: Enable Vertex AI Model Monitoring: detect data drift and adversarial distribution shifts
Feature Store write access restricted to data pipeline service account only (not developer accounts)
Statistical validation gate in training pipeline: reject training data with >2 sigma distribution shift
Model canary deployment: 1% traffic to new model for 48 hours with manual fraud analyst review
Maintain champion/challenger model architecture: never replace production model without A/B validation

threats.description: Vault root token stored in Terraform state file (tfstate) on GCS bucket without Object Versioning lock. CVE-2024-2048 affects Vault Enterprise <1.15.5 (cert auth method bypass). An attacker who compromises the Terraform service account can read the tfstate, extract the Vault root token, and access all secrets engines: transit (PAN encryption keys), database (dynamic AlloyDB/Spanner creds), PKI (Istio mTLS CA). Complete secret infrastructure compromise.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $200M -- total secret compromise, PCI CDE key exposure, forced re-encryption of all PAN data
mitigations: Upgrade Vault Enterprise to >=1.15.5 (patches CVE-2024-2048 cert auth bypass)
Rotate root token immediately and store in break-glass HSM procedure (not in tfstate)
Enable Terraform state encryption with GCP KMS and restrict bucket access to CI/CD SA only
Vault audit device: enable file + syslog audit backends with mandatory HMAC'd secrets
Vault Sentinel policies: enforce max TTL of 1 hour for all dynamic credentials

threats.description: Istio 1.21 PERMISSIVE mode misconfiguration on the settlement-service namespace (intended for migration, left in place). CVE-2024-23322 affects Istio <1.20.3 (bypassing peer authentication with direct pod IP access). An attacker in a compromised pod sends plaintext gRPC requests directly to the Settlement Service pod IP, bypassing Istio's mTLS enforcement. The attacker can impersonate the Trading Engine and submit unauthorized settlement instructions to DTCC.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $100M -- unauthorized settlement instructions to DTCC, systemic risk to clearing house
mitigations: Enforce Istio PeerAuthentication STRICT mode globally (no PERMISSIVE namespaces)
Upgrade Istio to >=1.21.1 (patches CVE-2024-23322 peer authentication bypass)
Istio AuthorizationPolicy: Settlement Service only accepts requests with source principal trading-engine SA
GKE Network Policy: deny all ingress to settlement pods except from Istio sidecar proxy ports
Continuous compliance scan: OPA Gatekeeper constraint rejecting PeerAuthentication mode=PERMISSIVE

threats.description: BigQuery dataset 'finserv_regulatory' has overly permissive IAM binding: allAuthenticatedUsers with bigquery.dataViewer role (misconfiguration from initial setup). Any GCP-authenticated user (including personal Gmail accounts added to the org) can query 5 years of trade history, customer PII (GLBA-protected), and SOX financial data. The BigQuery audit log shows this dataset was accessed 47 times in the last month by non-financial-team principals.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $45M -- GLBA privacy violation ($100K per incident), SOX material weakness, FINRA data governance failure
mitigations: Remove allAuthenticatedUsers binding immediately; restrict to finserv-regulatory-analysts@domain group
Enable BigQuery column-level security: mask customer_ssn, account_number, tax_id columns
Implement BigQuery row-level security: analysts only see data for their assigned accounts
VPC Service Controls: add BigQuery to the finserv perimeter (block exfil to non-perimeter projects)
BigQuery data loss prevention: Cloud DLP scan on scheduled queries, alert on PII in results

threats.description: The KYC/AML sanctions screening service uses pickle deserialization to load cached OFAC SDN list updates (performance optimization). CVE-2024-3651 (idna library DoS via crafted input) combined with Python pickle deserialization allows remote code execution if an attacker can poison the sanctions list cache in GCS. The attacker crafts a malicious pickle payload that executes os.system() on deserialization, gaining shell access to the KYC pod. From there, the attacker can whitelist sanctioned entities, enabling money laundering through the platform.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $500M+ -- BSA/AML violation, OFAC sanctions evasion, potential criminal prosecution, banking license revocation
mitigations: Replace pickle deserialization with JSON for all cached data (eliminate the RCE vector entirely)
Enable GCS Object Integrity Validation: CRC32C + MD5 checksums on sanctions list objects
Workload Identity: KYC pod service account has read-only access to sanctions GCS bucket
Container image hardening: distroless base image, read-only filesystem, no shell available
OFAC SDN list integrity: verify PGP signature from Treasury.gov before loading

threats.description: Spring Boot heap dump accessible via /actuator/heapdump endpoint. The Transaction Service processes PAN (Primary Account Number) data before tokenization via Cloud KMS. A heap dump during active card processing contains plaintext PAN, CVV, and cardholder names in memory. If the Actuator endpoint is exposed (see TM-FIN3-003), an attacker obtains a heap dump containing thousands of in-flight cardholder records, violating PCI DSS Requirement 3.4 (render PAN unreadable anywhere it is stored).
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $90M -- PCI Level 1 breach, card reissuance costs ($5-$25 per card x millions), brand damage
mitigations: Disable /actuator/heapdump and /actuator/threaddump endpoints entirely in PCI CDE services
Tokenize PAN at the earliest possible point (Cloudflare Workers edge tokenization before backend)
Use Java memory scrubbing: zero char arrays containing PAN after KMS tokenization call completes
GKE PodSecurityPolicy: deny SYS_PTRACE capability (prevent /proc/[pid]/mem reading)
PCI DSS 3.4.1: implement disk-level encryption (LUKS/dm-crypt) on all GKE node ephemeral storage

threats.description: SWIFT Alliance Access operator credentials are shared among 3 operations staff (credential sharing violates SWIFT CSP Mandatory Control 4.1). If one operator's credentials are compromised via phishing, the attacker can authorize fraudulent SWIFT MT103 payment messages. The Bangladesh Bank heist ($81M, 2016) used exactly this attack vector. Without individual accountability, forensic investigation cannot determine which operator initiated the fraudulent message.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $80M+ -- fraudulent wire transfers (Bangladesh Bank precedent: $81M stolen)
mitigations: Issue individual SWIFT operator credentials immediately (SWIFT CSP Mandatory Control 4.1)
FIDO2 hardware key (YubiKey) required for SWIFT message authorization (eliminate password-only auth)
Implement SWIFT Transaction Screening: ML-based anomaly detection on MT103 messages
Four-eyes principle: two separate operators must approve any payment >$1M
SWIFT Alliance Lite2: consider cloud-based SWIFT with improved access controls

threats.mitre_technique_name: Endpoint Denial of Service: Application or System Exploitation
threats.description: Go net/http HTTP/2 rapid reset vulnerability (CVE-2023-44487). The Market Data Service exposes a gRPC endpoint (HTTP/2) for real-time price feed distribution. An attacker sends HTTP/2 rapid reset frames (RST_STREAM immediately after HEADERS), consuming server goroutines without completing any request. With 100,000 rapid resets per second, all goroutines are consumed, and the Market Data Service becomes unresponsive. Trading Engine loses price feed, risk engine calculates stale VaR, and automated trading strategies execute with incorrect pricing.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $35M -- stale pricing leads to mis-hedged positions, regulatory best-execution failure
mitigations: Upgrade Go to >=1.21.3 / >=1.20.10 (patches CVE-2023-44487 HTTP/2 rapid reset)
Configure golang.org/x/net/http2 MaxConcurrentStreams=100 and MaxReadFrameSize=16384
Envoy sidecar (Istio): set http2_protocol_options.max_concurrent_streams=100
Circuit breaker: auto-switch to backup Bloomberg feed if primary Market Data Service latency >100ms
GKE Pod Disruption Budget: minAvailable=2 for market data pods during market hours

threats.mitre_technique_name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Bypass User Account Control
threats.description: Spring Security OAuth2 token validation uses a local JWT verification library that does not check the 'aud' (audience) claim. An attacker obtains a valid JWT from any OAuth2 client in the organization (e.g., internal HR portal), then replays it against the Account Service. The Account Service accepts the token because the signature is valid (same issuer), but the token was not intended for the trading platform. The attacker gains access to customer account data (balances, positions, PII) with a token meant for a completely different application.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $25M -- GLBA customer data breach, reputational damage, class-action lawsuit
mitigations: Enforce JWT audience (aud) claim validation: reject tokens where aud != 'securetrade-api'
Configure Spring Security: spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.audiences=securetrade-api
Implement token binding: bind JWT to client TLS certificate (OAuth 2.0 DPoP)
Short-lived access tokens (5-minute expiry) with refresh token rotation
API Gateway (Apigee): validate audience before forwarding to backend services

threats.mitre_technique_name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
threats.description: Settlement instruction messages (ISO 20022 pacs.008) are generated and sent to DTCC without cryptographic signing. The Settlement Service relies on network-level authentication (IP allowlist + client certificate) but does not sign individual message payloads. If a settlement instruction is disputed (wrong amount, wrong counterparty), the firm cannot cryptographically prove that the settlement instruction was generated by the authorized Settlement Service and not injected by a compromised intermediary. SEC Rule 17a-4(f)(3)(vii) requires message authentication.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $20M -- settlement dispute costs, DTCC penalty fees, regulatory investigation
mitigations: Implement XML Digital Signature (XMLDSig) on all ISO 20022 pacs.008/pacs.009 messages
Use HashiCorp Vault Transit engine for settlement message signing (HSM-backed key)
Maintain signed message archive in WORM storage for SEC 17a-4 retention (6 years)
DTCC CTM: enable bilateral message authentication for all trade confirmations
Audit Trail Service: log settlement instructions with full message hash before transmission

threats.mitre_technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
threats.description: Cloud KMS key 'pan-tokenization-key' is configured with IAM binding allowing the GKE node service account (not just the Transaction Service pod) to perform cloudkms.cryptoKeyVersions.useToDecrypt. Any pod on the same GKE node (including monitoring sidecars, log collectors, or compromised low-privilege services) can call KMS Decrypt via the node's ambient credentials. An attacker who compromises a non-PCI pod on the same node can decrypt all tokenized PAN data without ever touching the Transaction Service.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $150M -- PAN detokenization breach, PCI Level 1 assessment, card network fines
mitigations: Migrate KMS IAM from node service account to Workload Identity: only transaction-service SA gets decrypt
GKE Workload Identity Federation: bind KMS decrypt to specific Kubernetes ServiceAccount
Cloud KMS key rotation: enable automatic rotation every 90 days for pan-tokenization-key
VPC Service Controls: restrict KMS API access to VPC perimeter (no internet access to decrypt)
KMS audit: alert on any decrypt call from unexpected service account principals

threats.mitre_technique_name: Network Denial of Service: Direct Network Flood
threats.description: Layer 7 application DDoS targeting the trading API during market open (9:30 AM ET). Attacker uses a botnet to send 2M+ HTTPS requests/second with valid-looking JWT tokens (expired but syntactically correct) to the /api/v1/orders endpoint. Cloudflare WAF passes these requests because they are syntactically valid HTTP. The GCP backend processes JWT validation for each request, exhausting CPU on the Auth Service pods. Legitimate order flow is delayed >500ms, causing FINRA best-execution violations and triggering the market maker kill switch.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $25M -- trading halt during peak hours, lost spread revenue, regulatory fine
mitigations: Cloudflare Bot Management: enable JS challenge for non-API clients, API Shield for API clients
Move JWT expiry validation to Cloudflare Worker (reject expired tokens at edge, before GKE)
Cloudflare Rate Limiting Advanced: per-API-key rate limit with automatic blocking
GCP Cloud Armor Adaptive Protection: ML-based DDoS detection with auto-mitigation rules
Pre-provision 10x burst capacity for Auth Service pods during market hours (06:00-18:00 ET)

threats.mitre_technique_name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
threats.description: AlloyDB connection string with password hardcoded in Helm values.yaml committed to the GitLab repository. The repository is accessible to 50+ developers, including contractors with 90-day NDAs. CVE-2024-0985 (AlloyDB PostgreSQL REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW privilege escalation) allows an attacker with the hardcoded credentials to escalate from read-only analytics role to superuser, then exfiltrate all risk model parameters, portfolio positions, and proprietary trading algorithms from the AlloyDB columnar engine.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $40M -- proprietary algorithm theft (trade secrets), competitive disadvantage
mitigations: Rotate AlloyDB password immediately and remove from Helm values.yaml (use Vault dynamic secrets)
Upgrade AlloyDB to latest patch (fixes CVE-2024-0985 REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW escalation)
GitLab secret detection: enable SAST with gitleaks pre-commit hook, scan history with trufflehog
HashiCorp Vault database secrets engine: 60-second TTL dynamic credentials for AlloyDB
AlloyDB pgAudit extension: log all DDL and role changes, export to Chronicle SIEM

threats.mitre_technique_name: Modify Authentication Process: Multi-Factor Authentication Interception
threats.description: Adversary-in-the-middle phishing toolkit (evilginx2) proxies the FIDO2/WebAuthn authentication flow. The attacker sends a phishing email with a link to securetrade-login.com (typosquat). The proxy site forwards the WebAuthn challenge to the real securetrade.com, captures the signed assertion, and replays it. Even though FIDO2 is phishing-resistant by design, the proxy intercepts the session token AFTER successful WebAuthn authentication. The attacker obtains a valid session token for the trader's account.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $35M -- account takeover of high-net-worth trader, unauthorized trades, privacy breach
mitigations: Enable FIDO2 origin binding: WebAuthn rpId must exactly match securetrade.com (proxy cannot satisfy this)
Implement token binding (OAuth 2.0 DPoP) to bind session tokens to the client TLS certificate
Deploy certificate-based mutual TLS for all trader authentication (client certs not phishable)
Register securetrade-*.com typosquat domains proactively with Cloudflare Registrar
Real-time phishing domain monitoring: integrate with PhishTank, URLScan.io, and VirusTotal APIs

threats.description: SendGrid API key (SG.xxx) hardcoded in notification-service ConfigMap. CVE-2024-21626 (runc container escape) on an unpatched GKE node allows an attacker to escape the notification service container, read the ConfigMap from the Kubernetes API, and obtain the SendGrid API key. With SendGrid admin access, the attacker sets up email forwarding rules to intercept all trade confirmation emails, margin call notifications, and OTP codes sent to traders. Email-based account recovery becomes compromised.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $10M -- trader email interception, OTP bypass enabling account takeover
mitigations: Migrate SendGrid API key to GCP Secret Manager with Workload Identity access
Upgrade GKE nodes to containerd >=1.7.13 / runc >=1.1.12 (patches CVE-2024-21626)
SendGrid: enable IP access management -- restrict API calls to GKE egress NAT IP only
Implement app-layer OTP (TOTP via authenticator app) instead of email-based OTP
SendGrid webhook: alert on any email forwarding rule creation or API key usage from unexpected IP

threats.description: FINRA TRACE reporting gateway submissions are not logged with non-repudiable evidence. If FINRA disputes that a trade was reported within the 15-minute TRACE reporting window, the firm cannot produce cryptographic proof of the submission timestamp. The Reporting Service logs show 'submitted', but these application logs are mutable (stored on GKE persistent volume without write protection). FINRA Rule 7730 violation carries $25K per late report.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $5M -- FINRA late-reporting fines ($25K x 200 trades/day), reputational damage
mitigations: Timestamp all TRACE submissions with RFC 3161 trusted timestamping authority
Store TRACE submission receipts in WORM-configured GCS bucket (Object Lock, retention=7 years)
Cryptographic signature on each TRACE report payload before submission (RSA-SHA256)
Chronicle SIEM correlation: TRACE submission event must have matching network egress log to FINRA IP
Dual submission logging: both application log AND immutable audit trail entry for each TRACE report

threats.description: VPC Service Controls perimeter has an ingress rule allowing BigQuery Data Transfer Service from an external GCP project (data-analytics-sandbox-123). This project, created by a data science team for experimentation, is outside the security perimeter and has weaker IAM controls. An attacker who compromises the sandbox project can use the BigQuery Data Transfer ingress rule to exfiltrate regulated financial data from the production BigQuery dataset to the unprotected sandbox, bypassing all VPC SC data exfiltration protections.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $30M -- regulated data exfiltration to uncontrolled environment, GLBA violation
mitigations: Remove the ingress rule for data-analytics-sandbox-123 from VPC SC perimeter
Migrate data science workloads inside the VPC SC perimeter (use BigQuery authorized views)
VPC SC dry-run mode disabled: enforce all perimeter violations (no more permissive mode)
Access Context Manager: require device trust + corporate managed device for all perimeter access
GCP Organization Policy: constraints/gcp.restrictServiceUsage to prevent BigQuery in sandbox projects

threats.description: GKE Autopilot prevents direct node access, but CVE-2024-21626 (runc container escape via /proc/self/fd/7 race condition) allows a container with CAP_SYS_PTRACE to escape to the host. On GKE Autopilot, escaping to the node provides access to kubelet credentials and all pods' projected service account tokens. The attacker can then impersonate any pod's Workload Identity, including the Trading Engine (Spanner write) or Transaction Service (KMS decrypt). Full cluster compromise.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $200M -- full cluster compromise, all service identities stolen, total data breach
mitigations: Verify GKE Autopilot nodes running containerd >=1.7.13 (GKE auto-upgrades, confirm channel)
Enable GKE Sandbox (gVisor) for all PCI CDE pods (Transaction Service, Settlement Service)
Binary Authorization: attestation policy rejects any image built before the CVE-2024-21626 patch date
GKE Security Posture: enable workload vulnerability scanning, alert on CVE-2024-21626 in node images
Restrict container capabilities: drop ALL, add only NET_BIND_SERVICE where needed

threats.mitre_technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain
threats.description: Supply chain attack on container base images in Artifact Registry. An attacker compromises a GitHub Actions runner used for CI/CD and pushes a backdoored container image for the Trading Engine to Artifact Registry. The image contains a reverse shell that phones home to an attacker C2 server on port 443 (blends with HTTPS traffic). Binary Authorization is configured but the attestor private key is accessible to the compromised CI/CD runner, so the backdoored image passes attestation checks. The backdoored Trading Engine exfiltrates trade data in real-time.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $100M -- supply chain compromise, real-time trade data exfiltration, regulatory investigation
mitigations: Store Binary Authorization attestor key in Cloud KMS HSM (not accessible to CI/CD runner directly)
Multi-party attestation: require BOTH CI/CD attestation AND security team manual attestation for PCI images
SLSA Level 3 provenance: generate and verify build provenance attestations for all container images
Container image scanning: enable Artifact Registry Container Analysis, block deployment of images with CRITICAL CVEs
Network egress restriction: deny all outbound traffic from trading engine pods except to known endpoints (Spanner, Pub/Sub, KMS)

threats.description: Bloomberg B-PIPE data feed connection uses BLPAPI 3.23 over a dedicated leased line. While the leased line provides physical isolation, BLPAPI authentication uses a shared API key stored in the Market Data Service environment variable. If the Market Data pod is compromised, the attacker extracts the Bloomberg API key and connects from outside the leased line (Bloomberg allows internet-based B-PIPE connections with the same credentials). The attacker gains access to real-time Level 2 order book data, providing unfair market advantage (front-running capability).
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $20M -- market data theft enables front-running, FINRA market manipulation investigation
mitigations: Bloomberg IP allowlisting: restrict B-PIPE connections to the firm's egress NAT IP only
Store Bloomberg API key in HashiCorp Vault with 24-hour TTL (rotate daily)
Network segmentation: Market Data pod only allowed egress to Bloomberg IP range (cannot reach internet)
Bloomberg BSSO (Single Sign-On): use certificate-based auth instead of shared API key
Monitor Bloomberg connection audit logs for any connection from unexpected IP addresses

threats.description: Aurora PostgreSQL DR replica in us-east-1 has automated snapshots with default KMS key (aws/rds) instead of customer-managed CMK. AWS default KMS keys cannot have key policies restricting cross-account access. An attacker who gains access to the AWS account (via compromised IAM credentials) can share the Aurora snapshot with an external AWS account, then restore it to extract all financial data. The snapshot contains the full Spanner mirror: account balances, trade history, customer PII.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $60M -- full database snapshot exfiltration, PCI + GLBA breach notification
mitigations: Re-encrypt Aurora snapshots with customer-managed CMK (restrict key policy to finserv account only)
AWS SCP: deny rds:ModifyDBSnapshotAttribute for all principals (prevent snapshot sharing)
Enable AWS CloudTrail for all RDS API calls, alert on ModifyDBSnapshotAttribute events
Aurora IAM database authentication: eliminate password-based access (use IRSA tokens)
AWS Config rule: check that all RDS snapshots are encrypted with customer-managed keys

threats.mitre_technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
threats.description: An advanced attacker (nation-state APT) compromises a GCP project admin account and disables Cloud Audit Logs export to Chronicle SIEM. They modify the log sink filter to exclude data_access logs, then perform malicious operations (Spanner data exfiltration, KMS key export) that are no longer logged. The SOX ITGC audit trail is broken. The Security Command Center Premium findings for the compromised project are suppressed by setting a mute rule on the project. Detection blind spot lasts until the quarterly SOX control testing.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $75M -- undetected data breach, SOX audit failure, SEC enforcement action
mitigations: Organization-level log sinks: cannot be modified by project admins (managed at org node)
Immutable audit logs: export to WORM-configured GCS bucket in separate audit project
GCP Organization Policy: deny roles/logging.configWriter at project level
Chronicle detection rule: alert on any modification to log sink configuration
Dual-export: send audit logs to BOTH Chronicle SIEM AND an off-cloud SIEM (Splunk/Elastic)

threats.description: DNS hijacking via compromised Cloudflare account credentials. If the Cloudflare account admin password is phished (the account uses email+password authentication, not SSO), an attacker can modify DNS records for securetrade.com to point to a malicious server. All trader traffic is redirected to a phishing site that captures credentials and session tokens. DNSSEC validation only protects against third-party tampering, not authoritative record changes by the zone owner.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $50M -- mass credential theft, unauthorized trades from compromised accounts
mitigations: Enforce SSO for Cloudflare account access (eliminate email+password login)
Cloudflare: enable Zone Hold (prevents DNS record changes without explicit unhold + MFA)
Deploy CAA (Certification Authority Authorization) DNS records to prevent unauthorized TLS certs
Certificate Transparency monitoring: alert on any new certificate issued for securetrade.com
Registrar lock: enable Domain Lock at the registrar level to prevent unauthorized DNS transfers

threats.description: DTCC CTM (Central Trade Manager) connection uses SFTP with a static client certificate that has not been rotated in 18 months. If the DTCC client certificate private key is compromised (developer laptop theft, CI/CD pipeline exposure), an attacker can impersonate the firm's Settlement Service and submit fraudulent settlement instructions to DTCC. DTCC trusts the client certificate and processes the instructions. The T+1 settlement cycle means fraudulent settlements complete within 24 hours.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $75M -- fraudulent settlement instructions, counterparty losses, FINRA/SEC investigation
mitigations: Rotate DTCC client certificate immediately (18 months exceeds 12-month best practice)
Store DTCC private key in Cloud KMS HSM (FIPS 140-2 Level 3), not filesystem
Implement certificate pinning on the DTCC SFTP connection (detect MitM attempts)
DTCC dual-authorization: require two separate approvals for settlement instructions >$10M
Monitor DTCC connection logs for any connection from unexpected IP addresses or at unusual times

threats.description: The Audit Trail Service stores SOX-regulated records in GCS with Object Lock (WORM). However, the GCS bucket retention policy is set via Terraform with the same Terraform service account that manages other infrastructure. An attacker who compromises the Terraform SA can modify the retention policy (reduce retention period from 7 years to 1 day), wait for the retention to expire, then delete historical audit records. This destroys the SOX audit trail, preventing regulatory examination of historical trades.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $100M -- SOX audit trail destruction, SEC criminal referral, potential prison for officers
mitigations: GCS Bucket Lock: enable locked retention policy (once locked, cannot be reduced, even by project owner)
Separate Terraform SA for audit infrastructure (cannot modify production infra and audit infra with same creds)
GCP Organization Policy: deny storage.buckets.update for all principals on audit project
Dual-storage: replicate audit trail to AWS S3 Glacier with Object Lock in a separate AWS account
SEC 17a-4(f)(2)(ii) compliance: engage FINRA-approved third-party storage provider as backup

threats.component: Fraud Detection ML (Vertex AI Feature Store)
threats.description: Vertex AI Feature Store contains pre-computed customer behavioral features (average transaction amount, typical trading hours, geographic patterns, device fingerprints). These features constitute PII under GLBA and can be used to reconstruct detailed customer financial behavior profiles. The Feature Store IAM binding allows bigquery.dataViewer to the data science team (15 people), but the Feature Store is not covered by VPC Service Controls. A data scientist can export all customer features to a personal GCS bucket outside the perimeter.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $15M -- GLBA customer behavioral data exfiltration, privacy class-action
mitigations: Add Vertex AI Feature Store to VPC Service Controls perimeter (prevent cross-perimeter access)
Implement row-level security on Feature Store: data scientists only access features for assigned portfolios
Data anonymization: replace customer_id with irreversible hash in Feature Store (k-anonymity)
DLP inspection: scan Feature Store exports for PII patterns before allowing bucket writes
Audit log: alert on any Feature Store export operation to non-perimeter destinations

threats.mitre_technique_name: Adversary-in-the-Middle: LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay
threats.description: GCP External ALB SSL policy allows TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 (RESTRICTED profile not enforced, using COMPATIBLE profile). CVE-2014-3566 (POODLE) and CVE-2015-0204 (FREAK) attacks are possible against traders using older browsers. An attacker performs a TLS downgrade attack on the trader's connection, then decrypts the session to capture OAuth tokens, trade order data, and account information. PCI DSS 4.0 Requirement 4.2.1 mandates TLS 1.2+ for cardholder data transmission.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $10M -- TLS downgrade enables session hijacking, PCI non-compliance finding
mitigations: Change GCP ALB SSL policy from COMPATIBLE to RESTRICTED (TLS 1.2+ only, no CBC ciphers)
Enforce TLS 1.3 minimum via custom SSL policy: ECDHE+AESGCM cipher suites only
HSTS header: max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload (prevent any HTTP fallback)
Cloudflare: set minimum TLS version to 1.2 at edge (before traffic reaches GCP)
Certificate pinning in mobile trading app to prevent TLS interception

threats.description: Cloud Armor WAF rule bypass via HTTP request smuggling (CL.TE desync). An attacker sends a crafted request with conflicting Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding headers. Cloud Armor evaluates the request using Content-Length (sees benign payload), but the GKE backend (Envoy proxy) parses using Transfer-Encoding (sees the malicious payload). The smuggled request bypasses Cloud Armor SQL injection rules and reaches the Trading Engine with a SQLi payload in the order query parameter. The SQLi extracts trade data from Spanner.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $25M -- WAF bypass enables SQLi, trade data extraction, PCI CDE breach
mitigations: Enable Cloud Armor adaptive protection with request header normalization enabled
Envoy proxy configuration: reject requests with conflicting CL/TE headers (HTTP/1.1 strict mode)
Implement parameterized queries in ALL database access code (defense-in-depth against WAF bypass)
Cloud Armor: enable JSON parsing mode for API endpoints (detect payload in JSON body, not just query params)
Application-level input validation: allowlist characters for order parameters (alphanumeric + hyphen only)

threats.description: GCP Secret Manager IAM binding uses the secretmanager.secretAccessor role at the project level (not individual secret level). Any service account with this project-level binding can access ALL secrets in the project, including PCI CDE database passwords, SWIFT HSM PINs, Bloomberg API keys, and Vault unseal key. The principle of least privilege is violated. A compromise of any single pod's Workload Identity grants access to the entire secret inventory.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $120M -- all secrets compromised via single pod breach, cascading infrastructure compromise
mitigations: Migrate Secret Manager IAM bindings to individual secret-level (not project-level)
Each Kubernetes ServiceAccount (Workload Identity) accesses only its required secrets
Secret Manager: enable automatic rotation for all database credentials (30-day rotation)
Implement secret access audit: alert on any access from unexpected service account principal
PCI CDE secrets in separate GCP project (isolation: CDE project has no cross-project IAM)

threats.description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in the trading dashboard order entry form. The 'symbol' field accepts freeform text for custom OTC instrument names. React's JSX escaping is bypassed when rendering dangerouslySetInnerHTML for instrument descriptions fetched from the market data API. CVE-2024-43788 (webpack-dev-server XSS, affects development builds accidentally deployed to production). An attacker injects JavaScript that modifies displayed bid/ask prices, causing traders to execute orders at incorrect prices.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $15M -- price manipulation via XSS, erroneous trades, FINRA market manipulation claim
mitigations: Remove all dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage in trading components (use DOMPurify for any HTML rendering)
Content Security Policy: script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; no unsafe-inline/unsafe-eval
Ensure production builds do not include webpack-dev-server (separate Docker stages for dev/prod)
Input validation: restrict 'symbol' field to alphanumeric + period + hyphen (reject special characters)
Subresource Integrity (SRI) on all CDN-loaded scripts to prevent CDN compromise

threats.description: AWS Secrets Manager in the DR region (us-east-1) synchronizes secrets from GCP Secret Manager via a Lambda function running on a 5-minute cron. The Lambda function has an IAM execution role with secretsmanager:GetSecretValue on all secrets (Resource: *). The Lambda code logs the secret values at DEBUG level to CloudWatch Logs for troubleshooting (left from development). CloudWatch Logs are retained for 30 days with default encryption. Any IAM principal with logs:GetLogEvents can read all synchronized secrets from CloudWatch.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $80M -- all DR secrets exposed in CloudWatch Logs, PCI CDE credential leak
mitigations: Remove DEBUG logging from sync Lambda immediately (secrets NEVER logged at any level)
CloudWatch Logs: set retention to 1 day for the sync Lambda log group (minimize exposure window)
Encrypt CloudWatch Logs with customer-managed KMS CMK (restrict key policy to security team only)
Lambda IAM: scope secretsmanager:GetSecretValue to specific secret ARNs (not Resource: *)
Replace Lambda sync with AWS Secrets Manager cross-region replication (native, no custom code)

threats.mitre_technique_name: Network Denial of Service: Reflection Amplification
threats.description: Cloud Pub/Sub subscription backpressure misconfiguration. The Settlement Service Pub/Sub subscription has flow_control.max_outstanding_messages set to unlimited (default). During a flash crash or market halt event, the Trading Engine publishes 100,000+ trade events per second. The Settlement Service cannot process messages fast enough, Pub/Sub acknowledgement deadline expires, and messages are redelivered in a thundering herd. The Settlement Service OOM-kills repeatedly, creating a cascade failure that delays T+1 settlement processing beyond the SEC Rule 15c6-1 deadline.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $20M -- T+1 settlement deadline breach, SEC Rule 15c6-1 violation, counterparty penalties
mitigations: Set Pub/Sub subscriber flow_control: max_outstanding_messages=1000, max_outstanding_bytes=100MB
Dead-letter topic: messages that fail after 5 delivery attempts go to DLT for manual processing
Settlement Service: implement batch processing with configurable batch_size=100 and batch_timeout=5s
GKE HPA: scale Settlement Service pods based on Pub/Sub subscription/num_undelivered_messages metric
Circuit breaker: if unacked messages >50,000, pause Trading Engine publishing until backlog clears

threats.description: Grafana 10.3 dashboards display real-time trading metrics (order volume, P&L, position sizes) without authentication. The Grafana instance is deployed in the monitoring namespace with an Istio VirtualService that exposes it on an internal domain (grafana.securetrade.internal). However, a developer committed a VirtualService with hosts: ['*'] wildcard, making Grafana accessible via the external load balancer. Competitors or malicious actors can view real-time trading positions and strategies by accessing the Grafana URL.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $10M -- proprietary trading strategy exposure, competitive intelligence leak
mitigations: Fix Istio VirtualService: replace hosts: ['*'] with hosts: ['grafana.securetrade.internal']
Enable Grafana authentication: OAuth2 SSO with the firm's identity provider
GKE Network Policy: deny external ingress to monitoring namespace
OPA Gatekeeper: constraint to reject VirtualService with hosts containing wildcard
Grafana: configure anonymous access disabled and set admin password rotation to 30 days

threats.description: FINRA TRACE reports are submitted over HTTPS but the Reporting Service does not validate the TLS certificate of the FINRA endpoint (verify=False in Python requests library). An attacker who can perform DNS spoofing or BGP hijacking on the GCP egress path can intercept TRACE reports, modify trade quantities/prices to trigger a FINRA investigation against the firm, or suppress reports entirely to create late-reporting violations.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $8M -- FINRA data integrity investigation, potential sanctions
mitigations: Enable TLS certificate verification: requests.post(url, verify=True) with CA bundle
Pin FINRA TRACE endpoint TLS certificate in the Reporting Service configuration
Use GCP Private Google Access for FINRA endpoint (if available via interconnect)
Implement message-level signing: HMAC on TRACE report payload, verified by FINRA on receipt
Monitor egress traffic: alert if TRACE report traffic routes through unexpected AS paths

threats.mitre_technique_name: Endpoint Denial of Service: Application Exhaustion Flood
threats.description: The Trading Engine uses LMAX Disruptor ring buffer with a fixed size of 65536 (2^16). During extreme market volatility (Flash Crash scenario), legitimate order flow can reach 100,000+ orders per second. The ring buffer wraps around and overwrites unprocessed orders, causing order loss. Lost orders are not retransmitted (fire-and-forget design). Traders' orders disappear silently, violating FINRA Rule 5310 (best execution) and potentially SEC Rule 15c3-5 (market access risk controls).
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $50M -- lost orders during market crash, best execution violations, client lawsuits
mitigations: Increase Disruptor ring buffer to 1048576 (2^20) with sufficient JVM heap allocation
Implement back-pressure: Apigee returns HTTP 429 to clients when ring buffer occupancy >80%
Durable order queue: persist every received order to Pub/Sub before ring buffer insertion
Order acknowledgement: return orderId to client immediately, reject silently is never acceptable
Kill switch: FINRA 15c3-5 compliant automatic halt if unprocessed queue depth >threshold

threats.mitre_technique_name: Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Dependencies
threats.description: NumPy 1.26 and SciPy 1.12 are installed via pip from PyPI without hash verification. A supply chain attack (dependency confusion or typosquatting: numpy-utils instead of numpy) injects a malicious package that subtly modifies risk calculation results. The attacker alters the VaR (Value at Risk) Monte Carlo simulation to underreport risk by 15%, causing the firm to take on excessive risk positions that exceed regulatory capital requirements. The subtle nature of the manipulation means it passes code review and unit tests.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $300M -- underreported risk leads to excessive positions, potential firm insolvency during market downturn
mitigations: Pin all Python dependencies with hash verification in requirements.txt (pip install --require-hashes)
Use a private PyPI mirror (Artifact Registry Python repo) with allowlisted packages only
SLSA Level 2: verify build provenance for all Python packages (sigstore/cosign)
Statistical validation: compare risk model outputs against independent reference implementation weekly
Separate risk validation environment: run risk calculations independently and compare results

threats.description: WebAuthn credential lockout attack. An attacker repeatedly fails FIDO2 authentication with a valid username but invalid assertion (wrong authenticator). After 5 failed attempts, the Auth Service locks the account for 30 minutes (brute-force protection). The attacker systematically locks out all trader accounts (usernames enumerable via /api/v1/users endpoint that returns HTTP 200 for existing users and 404 for non-existing). During market open, all traders are locked out for 30 minutes, unable to manage positions during volatile market conditions.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $15M -- trader lockout during market hours, unable to manage risk positions
mitigations: Username enumeration fix: return consistent HTTP 200 for both valid and invalid usernames
Rate-limit authentication failures by source IP (not by username) to prevent targeted lockout
Progressive lockout: 1min, 5min, 15min delays instead of hard 30min lockout
CAPTCHA/proof-of-work challenge after 3 failed attempts from same IP (slow down automation)
Emergency bypass: phone-based OTP authentication when FIDO2 account is locked (out-of-band channel)

threats.description: Cross-cloud lateral movement via shared Kubernetes RBAC. The GKE-to-EKS DR failover uses a shared kubeconfig with cluster-admin role on both clusters (required for Velero backup restore). If an attacker compromises the Velero service account on GKE, they obtain cluster-admin on the EKS DR cluster as well. The EKS cluster has different security controls (no Binary Authorization, public API endpoint). The attacker uses EKS as a pivot point to access AWS resources (RDS Aurora, S3 backup buckets) that are more permissively configured than the GCP equivalents.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $60M -- cross-cloud lateral movement, DR environment becomes attack vector
mitigations: Separate Velero service accounts per cluster with minimum required RBAC (not cluster-admin)
Implement cross-cloud identity federation instead of shared kubeconfig (GCP-to-AWS OIDC)
EKS: mirror GKE security controls (enable Binary Authorization equivalent via OPA/Gatekeeper)
EKS: disable public API endpoint, match GKE private cluster configuration
Audit: alert on any cluster-admin usage on either cluster (both GKE and EKS)

threats.description: Twilio SMS sending uses the firm's registered sender ID for margin call notifications and OTP delivery. CVE-2024-22245 (Twilio SDK credential exposure in error messages) combined with a compromised Twilio API SID/Auth Token allows an attacker to send SMS messages from the firm's official sender ID. The attacker sends phishing SMS to traders: 'URGENT: Your account has been flagged. Verify at securetrade-verify.com'. Traders trust the message because it comes from the known sender ID, and enter credentials on the phishing site.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $8M -- trader credential theft via SMS phishing, account takeovers
mitigations: Rotate Twilio API SID and Auth Token, store in GCP Secret Manager with 30-day rotation
Twilio: enable Messaging Insights anomaly detection (alert on unusual SMS volume/patterns)
Trader communication policy: 'We will NEVER ask you to verify credentials via SMS'
Implement SMS message signing: include HMAC of trader account number (trader can verify authenticity)
Move OTP delivery to authenticator app (TOTP) -- eliminate SMS-based authentication entirely

threats.description: The Reporting Service uses DuckDB 0.10 for in-process analytical queries against Parquet files. CVE-2024-41672 (DuckDB arbitrary file read via COPY FROM) allows an attacker who can inject SQL into a report template to read arbitrary files from the container filesystem: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token (Kubernetes SA token), /proc/self/environ (environment variables with secrets), and mounted Secret Manager volumes. The attacker escalates from a report-viewing role to cluster-level access.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $12M -- privilege escalation via report template injection, SOX audit data exposure
mitigations: Upgrade DuckDB to >=0.10.2 (patches CVE-2024-41672 COPY FROM file read)
Parameterize all DuckDB queries -- never interpolate user input into SQL strings
Container security context: readOnlyRootFilesystem=true, drop ALL capabilities
Mount Kubernetes SA token with automountServiceAccountToken=false (inject only when needed)
Reporting Service: run DuckDB in a subprocess with seccomp profile restricting file system calls

threats.mitre_technique_name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Symmetric Encrypted Non-C2 Protocol
threats.description: GCP-to-AWS cross-cloud connectivity uses a site-to-site VPN (Cloud VPN to AWS VPN Gateway) for DR replication traffic. The VPN tunnel uses IKEv2 with a pre-shared key (PSK) that has not been rotated since initial deployment (14 months ago). The PSK is stored in both Cloud VPN and AWS VPN Gateway configurations, accessible to any engineer with compute.vpnTunnels.get or ec2:DescribeVpnConnections permissions. An attacker who obtains the PSK can decrypt captured VPN traffic (if they have network tap access), exposing all DR replication data: account balances, trade records, customer PII.
threats.financial_impact_estimate: $45M -- DR replication traffic decryption, PCI + GLBA breach
mitigations: Rotate VPN pre-shared key immediately (14 months exceeds 90-day rotation best practice)
Migrate from PSK to IKEv2 with certificate-based authentication (Cloud Interconnect + AWS Direct Connect)
Application-layer encryption: encrypt all replication data with Cloud KMS before VPN transmission
Restrict compute.vpnTunnels.get IAM permission to network admin team only (2 people)
Consider Google Cloud Interconnect Partner for dedicated private connectivity (no shared VPN)

attack_scenarios.name: Multi-Stage Trading Account Takeover via SSRF + Redis Session Hijack
kill_chain: 1. Exploit Next.js CVE-2024-34351 SSRF to steal GCP metadata service account token (TM-FIN3-001)
2. Use stolen SA token to read GCP Secret Manager (all secrets accessible per TM-FIN3-038)
3. Obtain Redis connection credentials, connect to Memorystore without ACL (TM-FIN3-006)
4. Inject forged admin session token for senior trader account
5. Execute unauthorized block trades through Trading Engine via hijacked session
6. Cover tracks by suppressing Chronicle SIEM alerts (TM-FIN3-031)
attack_scenarios.regulatory_impact: FINRA investigation, SEC enforcement action, potential criminal charges
attack_scenarios.detection_opportunity: Step 2 -- Secret Manager access from unexpected SA, if audit alerting is configured

attack_scenarios.name: Supply Chain Compromise via CI/CD to Trade Data Exfiltration
kill_chain: 1. Compromise GitHub Actions runner, push backdoored Trading Engine image (TM-FIN3-028)
2. Binary Authorization bypassed (attestor key accessible to CI/CD runner)
3. Backdoored container deployed to GKE Autopilot, reverse shell on port 443
4. Exploit CVE-2024-21626 runc escape to access host kubelet credentials (TM-FIN3-027)
5. Impersonate Transaction Service Workload Identity, call KMS Decrypt on PAN tokens (TM-FIN3-020)
6. Exfiltrate detokenized PAN data via DNS tunneling (bypasses VPC SC egress controls)
attack_scenarios.financial_impact: $200M -- PAN breach, card reissuance, brand destruction
attack_scenarios.regulatory_impact: PCI DSS Level 1 breach notification, card network fines, potential business closure
attack_scenarios.detection_opportunity: Step 1 -- Container image hash mismatch, if SLSA provenance is verified

attack_scenarios.name: Insider-Driven SOX Fraud via Spanner Tampering + Audit Destruction
kill_chain: 1. Rogue developer uses legitimate Spanner Writer IAM role to modify trade records (TM-FIN3-002)
2. Alter P&L attribution to hide $50M trading loss from SOX quarterly report
3. Compromise Terraform SA to modify GCS WORM retention policy on audit bucket (TM-FIN3-034)
4. Delete historical audit trail entries that would reveal the tampering
5. Modify AlloyDB analytics data to match tampered Spanner records (TM-FIN3-022)
6. SOX quarterly certification filed with materially false financial statements
attack_scenarios.financial_impact: $500M+ -- SOX fraud, stock price collapse, shareholder lawsuits
attack_scenarios.regulatory_impact: SEC criminal fraud referral, officer personal liability (SOX Section 302/906), potential imprisonment
attack_scenarios.detection_opportunity: Step 3 -- Terraform state change on audit bucket, if IaC drift detection is active

attack_scenarios.name: SWIFT Payment Fraud via Credential Compromise + DR Pivot
kill_chain: 1. Phish SWIFT operator shared credentials (TM-FIN3-016)
2. Lateral move to EKS DR cluster via shared Velero kubeconfig (TM-FIN3-047)
3. Access AWS Secrets Manager via DR Lambda CloudWatch Logs (TM-FIN3-040)
4. Obtain SWIFT Alliance Access credentials from DR secret store
5. Submit fraudulent MT103 wire transfers to attacker-controlled correspondent bank
6. Exploit 18-month-old DTCC client certificate for fraudulent settlement cover (TM-FIN3-033)
attack_scenarios.regulatory_impact: SWIFT CSP compliance failure, correspondent banking relationships severed
attack_scenarios.detection_opportunity: Step 1 -- SWIFT operator login from unexpected IP, if UEBA is configured in Chronicle

impacted_provisions.description: Notice and opt-out for NPI sharing

immediate_action_required: TM-FIN3-001 -- Next.js SSRF (CVE-2024-34351): Upgrade to >=14.1.1 TODAY
TM-FIN3-013 -- BigQuery IAM: Remove allAuthenticatedUsers binding TODAY
TM-FIN3-040 -- Lambda DEBUG logging: Remove secret logging from CloudWatch TODAY
TM-FIN3-038 -- Secret Manager IAM: Scope to individual secrets THIS WEEK
TM-FIN3-011 -- Vault root token in tfstate: Rotate and secure THIS WEEK
TM-FIN3-012 -- Istio PERMISSIVE mode: Enforce STRICT globally THIS WEEK
TM-FIN3-020 -- KMS node-level IAM: Migrate to Workload Identity THIS WEEK
TM-FIN3-050 -- VPN PSK rotation: Rotate 14-month-old key THIS WEEK
